title: Vision Language Models are Biased
authors: An Vo, Khai-Nguyen Nguyen et al. (KAIST, William & Mary, U. Alberta, Auburn)
contribution: VLMs achieve 100% accuracy on original images but ~17% on counterfactual modifications, proving they rely on memorized knowledge rather than actual visual analysis. Adopted by Google DeepMind (Gemini 3 Pro evaluation) and ByteDance (Seed1.8 evaluation). Integrated into lmms-eval framework.

title: The Illusion of Thinking
authors: Parshin Shojaee, Iman Mirzadeh et al. (Apple)
contribution: Large Reasoning Models face complete accuracy collapse beyond complexity thresholds on controllable puzzles; non-reasoning LLMs can outperform LRMs on simple tasks.
note: One of the most viral AI papers of 2025. Released before Apple WWDC. 3+ direct response papers.

title: The Illusion of the Illusion of Thinking (rebuttal)
authors: A. Lawsen (Open Philanthropy); v1 co-authored with C. Opus (Claude)
contribution: Apple's findings reflect experimental design limitations (token limits, mathematically impossible puzzle instances) rather than fundamental reasoning failures. First arXiv paper co-authored by an AI model.

title: Can VLMs Actually See and Read? (Modality Collapse Survey)
authors: Mong Yuan Sim, Wei Emma Zhang, Xiang Dai, Biaoyan Fang
contribution: Reviews 'modality collapse' -- VLMs rely on text while ignoring vision, yet still achieve competitive performance -- explaining why this occurs.

title: Breaking Down Video LLM Benchmarks
contribution: 26-35% of video benchmark questions answerable without video. Proposes VBenchComp pipeline exposing that benchmark scores overstate true video understanding.

title: VideoQA in the Era of LLMs
contribution: Video-LLMs falter on temporal reasoning and are paradoxically unresponsive to adversarial video perturbations while being highly sensitive to simple textual rephrasing.

title: Multimodal Hallucination Evaluation and Detection (Survey)
contribution: First comprehensive taxonomy of multimodal hallucination based on faithfulness and factuality across both Image-to-Text and Text-to-Image.

title: VIGIL: Hallucination Detection in Image Recontextualization
contribution: Granular hallucination detection pipeline using SAM 3, DINOv3, Qwen3-VL; goes beyond binary scores to textual error explanations.

title: Hallucination of MLLMs (Survey)
authors: Zechen Bai, Pichao Wang et al.
contribution: Layered classification tracing hallucinations to root causes with linked mitigation strategies. One of the most cited MLLM hallucination surveys.

title: Scaling Laws for Native Multimodal Models
contribution: Most extensive scaling study (457 trained models); early-fusion architectures match late-fusion at lower parameter counts, are more efficient, benefit from MoE with modality-agnostic routing. Challenges CLIP+LLM paradigm.
note: Highly cited; influenced Llama 4 and other early-fusion designs.

title: Emu3: Next-Token Prediction is All You Need
contribution: Multimodal model trained solely with next-token prediction (no diffusion, no CLIP); tokenizing images, text, and video into discrete tokens with a single transformer matches task-specific models in both generation and perception.

title: MMaDA: Multimodal Large Diffusion Language Models
authors: Ling Yang et al. (Princeton, Peking U., ByteDance)
contribution: First unified multimodal diffusion foundation model eliminating modality-specific components; introduces UniGRPO for unified RL across reasoning and generation. Outperforms LLaMA-3-7B in text and SDXL in images.

title: Unified Multimodal Understanding and Generation Models (Survey)
contribution: Categorizes 50+ unified multimodal models into three paradigms (diffusion-based, autoregressive, hybrid). Most complete taxonomy and timeline available.

title: Gemini 2.5: Pushing the Frontier with Advanced Reasoning, Multimodality, Long Context, and Agentic Capabilities
contribution: Gemini 2.5 technical report: multimodal understanding/reasoning and Agent capability SOTA.

title: Qwen2.5-VL Technical Report
contribution: Open-source multi-size multimodal model technical report.

title: InternVL3: Exploring Advanced Training and Test-Time Recipes for Open-Source Multimodal Models
contribution: Open-source multimodal model strong baseline reference.

title: MMMU-Pro: A More Robust Multi-discipline Multimodal Understanding Benchmark
contribution: Forced vision-text coupling causes significant score drops. Replacement benchmark for saturated MMMU.

title: MPBench: A Comprehensive Multimodal Reasoning Benchmark for Process Errors Identification
